provenance.source_corpus: Evaluating reintroduction methods for the Gulf of Saint Lawrence aster ( Symphyotrichum laurentianum ) on Prince Edward Island

source_spans.text: simulating potholes had a detrimental effect on populations.
simulating potholes had a detrimental effect on populations.
Competition for light can have negative influences on this plant's development
Competition for light can have negative influences on this plant's development
The creation of these pothole patches allows for increase in light availability

all factors which benefit SLA establishment and development
This was presumably due in large part to the frequent flooding
This was presumably due in large part to the frequent flooding and consistent waterlogging

nodes.name: SLA seed bank and germination success

source_spans.text: it is still possible that eelgrass deposition is a predominant factor that negatively affected the SLA seed bank and germination success
These accumulations of wrack have the ability to bury the seeds in the seed bank and inhibit their germination.
These accumulations of wrack have the ability to bury the seeds in the seed bank and inhibit their germination.

delays in germination resulting from exposure to low soil temperatures
transplants did not benefit from the removal of vegetation

nodes.name: seeding and the transplantation of greenhouse-grown seedlings

source_spans.text: Promising results related to seeding and the transplantation of greenhouse-grown seedlings at four in situ sites demonstrated that SLA plantlets have the potential to serve as seed stock to re-establish populations.

Promising results related to seeding and the transplantation of greenhouse-grown seedlings at four in situ sites demonstrated that SLA plantlets have the potential to serve as seed stock to re-establish populations.
The reduction in competition from other plants is believed to play an important role in SLA's establishment

ground temperature may not be optimal for SLA establishment throughout the germination season
